title: thymus albanus subvar. schefferi
intro: Thymus albanus subvar. schefferi, commonly known as the Balkan thyme or Alpine thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the Balkan region. It is a low-growing, woody herb with small leaves and pink or purple flowers, known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant is said to have antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. Thymus albanus subvar. schefferi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
